stage.profile_notes: Team time trial (first TTT opener since 1971). On TTT, stage-placement/holdbonus/late-arrival/etapebonus do NOT apply; value whole strong TT teams; captain best rider on the strongest TT squad.

team.teamName: The Times They Are A Changin'

decision.summary: Stage 1 is the 19.7km Barcelona TTT (Sat 4 Jul) and pre-stage-1 transfers are FREE. Composition and captain are HELD from the prior plan — nothing material changed — but the trade list is RESYNCED to the actual live roster: the 30-Jun maintenance incident already applied the Mads Pedersen→Kwiatkowski leg, so the live team is now Kwiatkowski, A.Yates, Vingegaard, C.Rodriguez, Politt, Affini, Tratnik, Simmons. Against that, the real delta is 5 sells (Yates, Vingegaard, Politt, Affini, Simmons) and 5 buys (Pogacar, Wellens, Jorgenson, Campenaerts, Cattaneo); Kwiatkowski, Rodriguez and Tratnik are already owned and held. Result: 2 riders on each of the four strongest TT squads — UAE (Pogacar+Wellens), Visma (Jorgenson+Campenaerts), Red Bull-BORA (Tratnik+Cattaneo), Netcompany INEOS (Rodriguez+Kwiatkowski) — ~49.5M spent, none flagged out. On a TTT only TTT placement scores, paid EQUALLY to every active rider of a top-5 team, so the EV engine’s per-rider e_stage does not model the stage and the cheap coverage rouleurs correctly show conf 0.00; I size the TTT from TT pedigree and use e_horizon only for the S2-S4 forward carry. Captain Tadej Pogacar: a free TTT inclusion who also carries the race’s highest-confidence ceiling (conf 1.00, captain_ceiling ~166k, e_horizon ~226k), is the S2 Montjuic favourite and an S3 contender — the certain right-tail over Jorgenson’s coin-flip 0.44-conf ceiling. Vingegaard is the deliberate cut: on a flat TTT his 14M buys no extra reward and forces min-price elsewhere; he’s re-bought free before the S3 summit. NOTE: Carlos Rodriguez (owned) has silently dropped out of this snapshot’s pool with NO isOut flag — treated as a feltet coverage gap, not a DNS, so he is kept; the pre-flight guard should re-verify his status ~hours before the start.

swaps.rationale: Anchor + captain. Buys into UAE (a TTT co-favourite) while carrying the race’s highest-confidence forward ceiling (live conf 1.00, e_horizon ~226k, captain_ceiling ~166k) as the S2 Montjuic punch-finish favourite and an S3 summit contender. Captaining a likely-TTT-winning UAE rider doubles his placement. Yates is the disposable UAE slot he replaces.

Second UAE slot to double up on a top TTT favourite (flat per-rider placement is high-ROI at 4.5M). Wellens is a puncheur who carries straight into the S2 hilly Montjuic finish, so the slot rolls forward with no churn. Replaces the min-price UAE rouleur Politt.

Keeps Visma (a TTT co-favourite) 2-up via a strong TT-engine rouleur who is also useful S2/GC. On a flat TTT, placement pays FLAT per top-5-team rider, so Vingegaard’s 14M buys no extra reward vs a cheap rouleur while it would force the other slots to rock-bottom prices; transfers are free now, so Vingegaard is better re-bought for the S3 summit (~1% fee) once stage odds confirm his role.

Second Visma slot. Campenaerts is an elite TT engine (former Hour-record holder) for only 4.0M — exactly the rider you want drilling a 19.7km TTT — keeping Visma at 2 on a top TT squad. Replaces the equivalent-price Affini.

Pairs with the kept Jan Tratnik to put 2 riders on Red Bull - BORA - hansgrohe, one of the four strongest TT squads. Cattaneo is a pure TT rouleur at 3.5M — cheap placement-scoring coverage on a podium-threat squad. Simmons (Lidl-Trek, off the four strong TT teams) is the sell that frees the slot. (conf 0.00 is expected: the EV engine cannot value flat TTT placement — a TT-pedigree coverage pick.)

teamB.lastDecisionMd:
# Welcome to Machine — cycle decision 2026-06-30T22:05:33.590Z

Stage 1 (ttt). Bank 0.0M, contractsLeft 8, current captain Paul Seixas.
Signal strength: enriched.matchRate = 0.6420454545454546 (low pre-race ⇒ EV is weak, picks lean on odds/price + ownership fade).

## Swap (forced ≥1 change)
- **SOLD Alex Aranburu** — e_horizon=43730, captain_ceiling=37044, gcOdds≈n/a, ownership=n/a%, value=4.0M, score=43.73 — lowest desirability among the 8 owned.
- **BOUGHT Florian Vermeersch** — e_horizon=43655, captain_ceiling=36942, gcOdds≈n/a, ownership=n/a%, value=3.5M, score=43.66 — forced least-bad legal swap (no clear upgrade in thin pre-race data); differential = fades ownership.

## Captain (always changed)
- **Paul Seixas → Isaac Del Toro** — e_horizon=88453, captain_ceiling=68811, gcOdds≈38.0, ownership=n/a%, value=9.5M, score=91.61 — highest captain-ceiling on the post-swap roster, ≠ previous captain.

## Plan written to plan-latest-B.json

findings.detail: Captain miss on 33% of stages (avg 29881 kr left; worst: flat (avg regret 46667)). Tighten captain selection on flat stages.

improve.mergeGateExplanation: Held (not merged): the conjunctive gate (in-sample ≥ MIN AND held-out ≥ MIN AND no fold regresses) was not met — held-out is -0.22% (must be positive / above floor); at least one cross-val fold regressed; no change proposed.

strategy.essence: Maximise total team VALUE GROWTH (kr) over the whole Tour. Strategy is EV TENDENCIES, not hard rules/quotas: build the squad by maximising Σ e_horizon under the constraints (8 riders, 50M budget, max 2 per real team, 1 captain), use roi_per_million to fill cheap breadth slots, tilt toward p_top15 breadth and lower ownership among near-equal e_horizon, and captain the owned rider with the highest captain_ceiling. GOLD = unlimited swaps; the only friction is the 1% buy fee, so only swap when the multi-stage gain beats it. Confidence governs aggression — discount low-confidence riders.
tendencies: Maximise Σ e_horizon under the hard constraints; do not churn for sub-1%-fee gains.
Use roi_per_million to fill cheap breadth slots so under-priced value is not overlooked.
Among near-equal e_horizon, tilt toward p_top15 breadth and lower ownership.
Captain the owned rider with the highest captain_ceiling (right-tail upside).
Concentrate variance/captaincy where stage leverage is high; prefer breadth on low-confidence stages.
Discount enriched.lowConfidence riders and fused.confidence < 0.4; matchRate governs how much to trust EV.
Respect profile_block / block_boundary look-ahead: do not dump a rider wanted again 1–3 stages later.
On TTT, stage-placement/holdbonus/etapebonus do not apply — value whole strong TT squads.

glossary.definition: Expected value in kr from THIS stage for a rider — Σ over placement, GC, jersey, sprint/KOM, TTT terms, minus hazard. Same kr axis the backtest scores.
Multi-stage expectation: e_stage plus discounted, hold-weighted look-ahead over the next 1–3 stages, net of the amortized 1% buy fee. The primary team-build objective.
e_horizon (net of amortized fee) per 1M kr of price — the efficiency metric for filling cheap breadth slots. Infinity if value is 0 with positive EV.
High/med/low tercile bucket of roi_per_million across the live (non-DNS) field — a quick-read value tag.
E[max(0, Δkr)] right-tail if this rider is captained this stage — the win windfall + placement upside + positive passive floor. Captain the owned rider with the highest one.
Fused stage-win probability (de-vigged odds + star/UCI/feltet signals via a weighted geometric pool), normalised so the board ~sums to 1.
Fused probability of finishing in the stage top-15 (p_stage expanded by a per-stage-type exponent). Drives etapebonus breadth.
Fused GC-strength: de-vigged GC win odds blended with a capped UCI-points prior. Paid every held stage as passive GC income.
HONEST 0–1 trust band = coverage × agreement (NOT a statistical interval). Below 0.4 the rider is flagged low-confidence; discount aggression.
Count of independent research channels that had a reading for this rider (0–5). More channels = sturdier fusion.
Fraction of the pool a LIVE source actually covered. Low pre-race (only GC odds live); rises ~24h out. Low matchRate = treat EV as weak.
Per-stage 0–1 decisive-stage weight (GC importance, volatility, summit, TT). Concentrate variance/captaincy where it is high.
Expected count of an optimal 8-man team’s riders inside the stage top-15. High on bunch days, low on selective mountain/TT days.
Grouping of consecutive similar stages + whether this stage closes the block — the 1–3 stage anti-churn look-ahead.
Holdbonus = real-team stage-finish spillover to all that team’s active riders. On TTT, stage-placement/holdbonus/late-arrival/etapebonus do NOT apply — value whole strong TT squads.
True when the dashboard successfully re-ran the pure fusion+EV engine to reconstruct per-rider numbers; false means it fell back to the snapshot name-lists.
